title: HTML Form Validation Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the built-in validation features of HTML5 forms.

questions.question: Which attribute ensures that a field cannot be left empty upon submission?
options: validate
must-fill
required
non-empty

questions.question: The <input type='email'> provides which type of automatic validation?
options: It checks if the email is currently active.
It ensures the input contains an @ symbol and a domain name.
It prevents the user from entering more than 20 characters.
It encrypts the email address before submission.

questions.question: Which attribute should you use to limit the maximum number of characters allowed in a text input?
options: limit
count
maxlength
size

questions.question: What is the primary role of the 'pattern' attribute in an input tag?
options: To set the background pattern of the input field.
To define a Regular Expression (RegEx) that the user's input must match.
To specify the font family for the input text.
To determine the layout pattern of the entire form.

questions.question: Is client-side HTML validation sufficient for securing a web application?
options: Yes, it is the most secure method available.
No, it's for user experience; server-side validation is mandatory for security.
Only if you use the 'pattern' attribute.
Only if the user is on a mobile device.
